speaker: MATT
raw_text: He goes and drags him back a little bit, begins rifling through whatever it might have had, and looks over right at Frumpkin. Grabs the trident spear and throws it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We should wake everyone up!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Wake up!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ow!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Wake up!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Fjord! (cawing)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What, what?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Don't wake up Kiri, though. She deserves a really nice night's sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: She really does, let her sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She's all curled up in the corner, like (trilling) with her feathers all poofed up.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to give her my blanket.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to send Frumpkin to crawl down and get a look.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, roll a stealth check for Frumpkin.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Does Frumpkin fall down the tunnel, then?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Cats can do some pretty crazy shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It’s fine. Good, four.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Frumpkin drops down onto the soft and makes it around. Focusing on it, you can see that another merrow has emerged into that chamber and-- nope.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We didn't hide the bodies.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Goes around and sees the bodies there lying in the middle of the space. He (growls).

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, we left the corpses out.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oops.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay, so as you get up on land, you focus and looking around side-to-side, make sure that you're aware of everything around you and get ready to defend and deflect.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The first attack--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you want to move around to get onto land, otherwise you're going to be having a hard time in the water here attacking them because you're constantly swimming.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Can I do that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You still have movement, yeah. So you can move five, ten, 15, 20, 25 there. So you manage to whip around, get out, up onto land and not end up with a disadvantage on your attacks.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, the first one is 15?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 15 does hit, yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Woohoo! Okay, second attack; not as good, 12.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 12 does not hit, unfortunately. But the first strike, as you pull up, cracks it right in the jaw. The next one, it manages to bash your arm out of the way and it deflects off the side-- no impact.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, that's seven damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty, the first strike.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And then I am going to take my bonus and spend a ki point to do Patient Defense.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: If you guys want to take a short rest, if you guys are inspecting the rest of the area.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you look at the trident, it looks like it's a very tightly woven rope, but the individual threads of rope are treated like a leather-type stat, so it's a really tight leather cord.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is it attached to his person in some way?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: From what you can see, where it was dangling from there, they’re wrapped in a loose loop around the side of their armor, and when they throw it, they catch the end of it and pull it back.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll take the trident and unhook it from his armor.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. It's about 60 feet of leather cord, if you want to call it that. Sturdy leather cord. It's heavy, so it's about twice the weight of hemp rope. It's stronger, but it's heavier.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: These guys were big.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, they're pretty strong.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Can I take a short rest while everyone's doing this?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You recognize a majority of the iconography here. It looks like one of these bundles-- one of these boxes that was gathered here-- was filled with holy symbols and religious iconography, most of which appear to be deities that are outlawed by the Empire. You see symbols and worship idols for the Archeart. You see some for Avandra, the Changebringer. You see a number of Moonweaver holy symbols with the two crescent moons across the silver cross. You see the hook and the wreath of the Wildmother, Melora, a number of those scattered about, some in disrepair, with actual pieces of dried barley and other grains around it. You see a number of symbols of Kord, the Stormlord. You see a handful for the Everlight. There are a few more dubious symbols as well. There's one that looks like a dark, horned crown that you recognize that is used to invoke Asmodeus, the Lord of the Hells. You see one that looks like-- you only see a few of these, but it looks like a series of chains and shackles that are across a coffin-like box, and you know that, because you rolled super high, to represent Bane, the Strife Emperor, who is a deity of conquest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Natural 19, for 21.
